sanskrit: क्षिप्रं भवति धर्मात्मा शश्वच्छान्तिं निगच्छति । कौन्तेय प्रतिजानीहि न मे भक्तः प्रणश्यति ॥
hindiTranslation: वह तत्काल उसी क्षण धर्मात्मा हो जाता है और निरन्तर रहनेवाली शान्तिको प्राप्त हो जाता है ।  हे कुन्तीनन्दन  तुम प्रतिज्ञा करो कि मेरे भक्तका विनाश पतन नहीं होता ॥
englishTranslation: One who is devoted to Me quickly becomes righteous and attains lasting peace. O son of Kunti, declare it boldly: My devotee never perishes.
essence: Devotion quickly changes a person and protects them from ruin.
speaker: Krishna
simpleMeaning: A devoted person quickly becomes upright and finds lasting peace. Krishna says his devotee will not come to ruin.
simpleInsight: Devotion is not fragile. It steadies you and gives the courage to trust the path.
detailedMeaning: On the battlefield of Kurukshetra, Arjuna is frozen by grief and doubt. Krishna answers with reassurance: devotion changes a person quickly, brings lasting peace, and never leaves a true devotee to perish.
modernRelevance: You are standing at the hospital corridor after bad news, unsure whether you can keep going. This verse says devotion does not leave you stranded; it steadies you when fear says collapse.
translationLiteral: क्षिप्रम् (quickly) / भवति (becomes) / धर्मात्मा (righteous in nature) / शश्वत् (everlasting) / शान्तिम् (peace) / निगच्छति (attains) / कौन्तेय (O son of Kunti) / प्रतिजानीहि (declare boldly) / न (not) / मे (My) / भक्तः (devotee) / प्रणश्यति (perishes)
insight: It shifts the mind from fear of collapse to trust in a sustaining presence.

quoteVariants.text: Devotion remakes you fast. Peace follows, and the devoted are never lost.
I won’t be abandoned if I stay devoted.
